title: Hydra active workers state
description: Runtime state of in-flight workers. Commander writes this after each spawn and on each worker completion. See CLAUDE.md 'Operating loop' step 6 (Track) — worker entries MUST carry {id, ticket, repo, tier, started_at, subagent_type, status} and SHOULD carry {worktree, branch} (optional for back-compat with pre-2026-04-17 entries; see docs/specs/2026-04-17-active-schema-drift.md).

id.description: Stable worker id used by Agent / SendMessage / TaskStop.

repo.description: Repo slug (owner/name) or the local_path from state/repos.json.

started_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when the worker was spawned.

subagent_type.description: Which subagent type spawned this worker. Must match a file in .claude/agents/.

status.description: Current lifecycle state. Both 'paused_on_question' (underscored) and 'paused-on-question' (hyphenated) are accepted for one release cycle — see docs/specs/2026-04-17-active-schema-drift.md for the deprecation plan; CLAUDE.md uses the hyphenated form. 'rescued' is set by the worker timeout watchdog when partial work is recovered via scripts/rescue-worker.sh.

worktree.description: Absolute path to the worker's git worktree. Optional for back-compat with in-flight entries from before 2026-04-17 (ticket #82); new entries SHOULD set it so scripts/rescue-worker.sh has a canonical source instead of reconstructing the path from convention.

branch.description: Branch the worker is committing to, e.g. 'hydra/commander/82' or the 'commander/hydra-82' fallback when the preferred pattern is taken. Optional for back-compat; new entries SHOULD set it so the watchdog does not have to guess between the two naming patterns.

last_updated.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of the most recent write, or null if never touched.
